blurb:
Evie is eight years old, 49 pounds of pure sweetness, and completely deaf. You'd never know it though. She's incredibly attentive and responds to everything around her like she can hear every word you're thinking. Being deaf hasn't slowed her down at all. She's smart, social, and bonds quickly with people.

She loves kids, gets along with other dogs after slow introductions, and is motivated by treats and toys. That food motivation makes her eager to learn new routines, which is handy since she's already housebroken and crate trained. She doesn't need a big backyard to be happy. What she really wants is family time and plenty of cuddles.

Evie's had some tough luck but she's ready to be someone's whole world. She's spayed, fully vaccinated, and microchipped. If you've got room in your heart for a loyal, loving companion who just happens to navigate the world a little differently, Evie might be exactly what you're looking for.

Barkville Dog Rescue is in Roswell. Email them or submit an application online to meet this beautiful girl.
scoring_notes:
• Deaf dog overcoming disability creates inspiring special needs story
• Well-trained senior appeals to adopters seeking grateful, settled companion
• Longer shelter stay suggested by 'tough luck' reference

blurb:
Annalise is a grey and white Alaskan Malamute who's probably around nine or ten years old. She was an owner surrender, likely used for breeding, and ended up out of time at a high kill shelter before Releashed stepped in. She's got that classic senior dog wisdom mixed with a surprising playful streak when the mood hits.

She loves other calm male dogs but won't tolerate the crazy ones, which honestly makes perfect sense. She's fearful and barks at new people initially, but once she gets comfortable, her sweet personality comes through. She's perfectly content to sit on the couch and watch TV with you, which sounds like an ideal Friday night.

Annalise needs a quiet home without children and someone who understands she'll need daily medication for a treated medical condition. She'd do best with patient owners who can give her time to settle in and feel safe. If you've got the room and the right calm energy, this lovable girl could be exactly what your home is missing.

Releashed Rescue is based in Roswell. Email them to learn more about Annalise and the adoption process.
scoring_notes:
• Breeding mill survivor to death row rescue creates powerful backstory
• Senior Malamute with special needs appeals to dedicated rescue community
• High-kill shelter background suggests very long journey to safety

blurb:
Charlotte has been waiting in foster care for nearly six years. She's a fantastic cat who loves people, but she's shy when she first meets you. Once she warms up though, she's a big cuddle bug who will stick close to wherever you are.

She gets along well with other cats as long as they don't chase her around. She just wants to do her own thing without being bothered. She has mild allergies that her foster mom helps manage, but that's not complicated. Just something to know about.

Six years is a long time to wait for someone to notice you. She's house trained, good with other cats, and ready to finally have a home that's really hers. The Cats Meow can tell you exactly what her allergy management looks like day to day.

**The Cats Meow**
Atlanta, GA 30339
Info@catsmeowrescue.org
scoring_notes:
• Six-year wait creates urgent, heartbreaking narrative perfect for newsletter feature
• Shy-to-cuddly transformation story resonates with patient adopters
• Longest wait time demands immediate community attention

blurb:
Roscoe is an eight-year-old Boxer mix with the kind of personality that makes you wonder why he's still looking for a home. He's 54 pounds of happy, social energy wrapped up in a dog who genuinely loves people. He's got that perfect medium-low energy level where he'll play with toys or go for a walk, then settle right in for couch time.

He's dog-friendly and would love a buddy to play with, but he'd be just as content being the center of attention. He's already figured out how to be a great housemate. Housebroken, crate trained, walks well on leash, and knows basic manners. He's looking for adults only or a family with kids 12 and up.

Roscoe's the kind of dog who wants to be wherever you are. Whether that's playing in the yard, going for walks, or just hanging out while you watch TV, he's going to be your loyal shadow. He's neutered, fully vaccinated, and microchipped, so he's ready to go home today.

Barkville Dog Rescue is in Roswell. Email them or submit an application online to meet this sweet boy.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect personality with mystery of why still available creates reader intrigue
• Ideal family dog characteristics appeal to broad newsletter audience
• 'Still looking' language suggests extended but not extreme wait time

blurb:
Molly used to be feral at a local car shop until she slowly earned her foster parent's trust. She disappeared for a few weeks and showed back up pregnant, had four kittens on the 4th of July, and now all her babies have been adopted. Time for mom to find her own home.

She's not your normal cat. She loves belly rubs and will pretty much let you do whatever you want with her, though she might give you a little back chat about it. She's not the most playful, but she loves to be cuddled and will spend hours gazing out the window in a sunny spot. When she's hungry, she'll tell you with a very quiet meow, nothing aggressive.

She's good with kids and adults, doesn't mind dogs, and is fully litter box trained. She's spayed and has her rabies vaccine. After raising four kittens and living rough for a while, she's ready for the quiet life where someone appreciates a sweet, low-maintenance companion.

**Releashed Rescue**
P.O. Box 767566, Roswell, GA 30076
releashedrescue@gmail.com
scoring_notes:
• Feral-to-mother transformation creates powerful redemption narrative
• Low-maintenance personality appeals to busy families seeking easy companion
• Post-kitten-raising timing suggests moderate shelter stay

blurb:
Judy is eight months old with a sleek coat, a big purr, and a little heart hiccup that doesn't slow her down one bit. She was born with a small hole between the chambers of her heart, but she's had two echocardiograms and is thriving. The vet can tell you all about it, but honestly, she has no idea anything is different about her.

Playtime is her love language. Feather wands, string toys, anything that moves. She'll play independently too, but she especially loves it when you join in. When she's not busy having fun, you'll find her draped across your lap in the cutest half-on, half-off cuddle nap or snoozing in a sunny window. She got along great with her siblings and the adult cats in her temporary foster home, so another kitty friend would make her happy.

She's fully vetted and ready to go. She'd do best with gentle kids who understand how to play respectfully with a kitten. Mostly Mutts can tell you exactly what her heart condition means for her long-term care, but right now she's just a normal kitten living life to the fullest.
scoring_notes:
• Heart condition creates compelling medical backstory with positive outcome
• Young playful kitten appeals to broad adopter base despite health needs
• Recent arrival with strong adoption prospects
